admissions_process.summary: GU publishes a graduate admissions flow centered on the online application, document review, and program selection for master's applicants. No separate graduate admissions manual was recovered beyond the official pages listed here.
steps: Review the official Graduate Programs page and the selected graduate department page.
Submit the graduate application form with the required supporting documents.
Provide any required credential equivalence documents for prior study.
Follow any program-specific guidance published by the relevant department.

required_documents.summary: GU publishes a standard graduate document checklist on the Graduate Programs / Graduate Admissions page.
items: Certified copy of Lebanese Baccalaureate or equivalent
Bachelor's degree from a recognized institution of higher learning
Certified copy of bachelor's degree equivalence
Passport or national identity card for Lebanese applicants
Family civil status record for Lebanese applicants
NSSF declaration form for students under 30
Valid residence permit copy for non-Lebanese applicants

language_requirements.summary: No standalone official graduate language-threshold page was recovered in the reviewed GU source set.
notes: The reviewed graduate pages do not publish a TOEFL, IELTS, or equivalent cutoff.
Program curricula and catalogue materials include English and Arabic course content, but no numeric admission threshold was recovered.

tuition_model.summary: GU publishes 2025-2026 graduate tuition of 170 USD per credit hour for the MBA, Master of Education, and Master in Arabic Language and Literature. The Financial Policies page also lists a 'PHD Arabic' tuition row, but no official PhD program evidence was recovered, so that row is not modeled as a program.
notes: Program-level tuition remains null in research/gu/programs.json because tuition is centralized here.
No official numeric graduate tuition row was recovered for the Islamic Studies master in the reviewed source set.

tuition_table.notes: The Financial Policies page lists Masters of Business Administration tuition at 170 USD per credit hour.
The Financial Policies page lists Masters of Education and Masters of Arabic Literature tuition at 170 USD per credit hour.

application_fee.notes: The graduate admissions page lists a non-refundable 50 USD application fee.

registration_fees.notes: The Financial Policies page lists a 75 USD registration fee for Summer.
The Financial Policies page lists a 150 USD registration fee for Fall and Spring.

fee_structure.notes: The official fee page also includes a first-payment schedule, a deferred-payment contract, and a non-refundable 20% seat-reservation deposit.

payment_methods.summary: No explicit graduate payment-method matrix was recovered in the reviewed GU pages.
notes: The reviewed sources document the fee schedule and deferred-payment process, but not a separate payment-method table.

payment_plans.summary: GU publishes a deferred-payment process and a semester-based first-payment schedule.
items: Students requesting installments complete a deferred payment form.
Accepted students sign a deferred payment contract.
First payment is 500 USD in Fall and Spring.
First payment is 50% of total fees in Summer.
Accepted students pay 20% of semester tuition to reserve a seat.
Graduate and Special Students have no refund available.

scholarships.summary: The Financial Policies page includes a Financial Aid & Scholarships section, but the linked aid file is external and was not retained in the official GU URL set.
notes: No graduate scholarship award amounts were recovered from official GU URLs only.

financial_aid.summary: The official Financial Policies page references financial aid, but no graduate aid amounts or award rules were recovered from the official GU URL set.
notes: The linked aid file is external to the GU domain and was excluded from the official-source set.

academic_calendar.summary: GU publishes a 2026-2027 academic calendar covering registration periods, semester starts, late registration or add-drop periods, withdrawal deadlines, final exams, and holidays.

graduate_regulations.summary: GU's catalogue and Financial Policies page centralize graduate academic policies, grading, graduation, advising, course syllabus requirements, tuition and fee policies, transfer or articulation rules, refund policy, and withdrawal policy.

international_students.summary: GU publishes non-Lebanese applicant document requirements on the graduate admissions page, but no broader standalone international-student policy page was recovered.

accreditation.summary: GU's Accreditation & Equivalence page states that its undergraduate and graduate degrees are authenticated or accredited by the Directorate of Higher Education in Lebanon.
items: Graduate degrees authenticated or accredited by the Directorate of Higher Education in Lebanon
notes: The page also lists master's degree names, including Business Administration, Education, Arabic Language and Literature, and Islamic Studies, as accreditation context.
